message:
test(fcp-conformance): pin fcp-host batch invoke + MCP JSON-RPC wire contracts

Add two new conformance suites covering the agent-facing host control
surface, and re-resolve Cargo.lock so the existing `futures-util`
workspace dep that fcp-conformance declares actually appears in the
locked dependency graph (previously absent because no current test
referenced it).

host_batch_invoke_contract_conformance.rs:
  Exercises `fcp_host::BatchInvokeRequest`, `BatchInvokeResponse`,
  `BatchOperation`, `BatchOptions`, `BatchStatus`, `OperationResult`,
  `OperationResultStatus`, and `BatchOperationError`. Pins:
   - documented BatchOptions defaults: max_parallelism=8,
     stop_on_first_error=false, timeout_ms=30_000
   - per-operation dependency-metadata JSON shape
   - aggregate status string vocabulary
   - which OperationResult / BatchOperationError fields are
     `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = ...)]` so missing-vs-null on the
     wire stays deterministic.

host_mcp_jsonrpc_contract_conformance.rs:
  Exercises `McpJsonRpcRequest`, `McpJsonRpcResponse`,
  `McpJsonRpcError`, `McpJsonRpcErrorResponse`, and `McpErrorCode`.
  Pins:
   - the four standard JSON-RPC error codes (-32600, -32601, -32602,
     -32603) and their canonical English messages.
   - the three FCP-specific MCP error codes
     (-32001 ToolNotFound, -32002 ToolExecutionError, plus the third
     listed below) and which of those codes are considered "standard
     JSON-RPC" vs MCP-specific via the bool tag in the table.

Cargo.lock: only diff is `+ "futures-util",` under the
fcp-conformance dependency block, matching the workspace dep already
declared in `crates/fcp-conformance/Cargo.toml`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
